Constraint Types Explained\n292\t\n293\tCargo uses several version requirement syntaxes:\n294\t\n295\t| Syntax | Meaning | Example |\n296\t|--------|---------|---------|\n297\t| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x version (caret requirement) | `serde = \"1\"` accepts 1.0.0 through 1.x.x |\n298\t| `\"0.3\"` | Any 0.3.x version (caret requirement for 0.x) | `futures = \"0.3\"` accepts 0.3.0 through 0.3.x |\n299\t| `\"0.3.0\"` | Exact match (rarely used) | Pinning to exact patch version |\n300\t\n301\t**Default Behavior:** Cargo uses caret requirements (`^`) by default:\n302\t- `^1` → `>=1.0.0, <2.0.0`\n303\t- `^0.3` → `>=0.3.0, <0.4.0`\n304\t\n305\t**NEEDLE Practice:** Most dependencies use the default caret requirement through simple version strings like `\"1\"`, `\"0.3\"`, etc.\n306\t\n307\t---\n308\t\n309\t## Rust Compiler Version\n310\t\n311\t| Component | Minimum Version | Source |\n312\t|-----------|----------------|--------|\n313\t| **Rust Edition** | 2021 | NEEDLE requires Rust 1.75 or higher\n319\t- Uses Rust 2021 edition\n320\t- All dependencies must be compatible with Rust 1.75+\n321\t\n322\t**Verification:**\n323\t```bash\n324\trustc --version  # for each dependency\n347\t2. **Query registry** → Find latest versions satisfying all constraints\n348\t3. **Check Cargo.lock** → Use locked versions if present (reproducible builds)\n349\t4. **Resolve conflicts** → Find compatible version set for all dependencies\n350\t5. **Write Cargo.lock** → Lock exact versions for reproducibility\n351\t\n352\t**NEEDLE Status:** Cargo.lock is committed |\n|--------|---------|---------|\n| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x version (caret requirement) | `serde = \"1\"` accepts 1.0.0 through 1.x.x |\n| `\"0.3\"` | Any 0.3.x version (caret requirement for 0.x) | `futures = \"0.3\"` accepts 0.3.0 through 0.3.x |\n| `\"0.3.0\"` | Exact match (rarely used) | Pinning to exact patch version |\n\n**Default Behavior:** Cargo uses caret requirements (`^`) by default:\n- `^1` → `>=1.0.0, <2.0.0`\n- `^0.3` → `>=0.3.0, <0.4.0`\n\n**NEEDLE Practice:** Most dependencies use the default caret requirement through simple version strings like `\"1\"`, `\"0.3\"`, etc.\n\n---\n\n## Rust Compiler Version\n\n| Component | Minimum Version | Source |\n|-----------|----------------|--------|\n| **Rust Edition** | 2021 | Cargo.toml line 4 |\n| **Rust Version** | 1.75 | Cargo.toml line 5 |\n| **MSRV (Minimum Supported Rust Version)** | 1.75 | `rust-version = \"1.75\"` |\n\n**Constraints:**\n- NEEDLE requires Rust 1.75 or higher\n- Uses Rust 2021 edition\n- All dependencies must be compatible with Rust 1.75+\n\n**Verification:**\n```bash\nrustc --version  # Should show >= 1.75\n```\n\n---\n\n## Feature Dependencies\n\nNEEDLE uses Cargo features to conditionally compile dependencies:\n\n| Feature | Dependencies Enabled | Default |\n|--------|---------------------|---------|\n| **otlp** | opentelemetry, opentelemetry_sdk, opentelemetry-otlp, opentelemetry-semantic-conventions, tonic, tracing-opentelemetry | ✅ Yes |\n| **integration** | otlp + testcontainers | ❌ No |\n\n**Default Feature:** `otlp` is enabled by default (line 26: `default = [\"otlp\"]`)\n\n---\n\n## Dependency Resolution Order\n\nCargo resolves dependencies in this order:\n\n1. **Read Cargo.toml** → Get minimum version constraints for each dependency\n2. **Query registry** → Find latest versions satisfying all constraints\n3. **Check Cargo.lock** → Use locked versions if present (reproducible builds)\n4. **Resolve conflicts** → Find compatible version set for all dependencies\n5. **Write Cargo.lock** → Lock exact versions for reproducibility\n\n**NEEDLE Status:** Cargo.lock is committed → **reproducible builds guaranteed**\n\n---\n\n## Compatibility Integration\n151\t\n152\t### NEEDLE System Overview\n153\t\n154\tNEEDLE (Navigates Every Enqueued Deliverable, Logs Effort) is the bead management system that ARMOR uses for task tracking. Pluck is the primary strand within NEEDLE that handles bead selection from assigned workspaces.\n155\t\n156\t### NEEDLE/Pluck Versions\n157\t\n158\t| Component | Version | Binary Location | Purpose Every Enqueued Deliverable, Logs Effort) is the bead management system that ARMOR uses for task tracking. `CGO_ENABLED=0` - Disable CGO for static binary\n74\t  - `GOOS=linux` - Target Linux\n75\t  - `-ldflags=\"-s -w\"` - Strip debug information for smaller binary\n76\t- **Binary Location:** `/armor` (in container)\n77\t\n78\t## Dependency Management\n79\t\n80\t### Go Modules\n81\t- **Module Path:** `github.com/jedarden/armor`\n82\t- **Key Dependencies:**\n83\t  - ARMOR\n\n**Bead:** bf-62zau  \n**Date:** 2026-07-09  \n**Project:** ARMOR (Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository)\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document captures the current versions of all development tools used in the ARMOR project. ARMOR is a Go-based S3-compatible proxy server that provides transparent encryption for Backblaze B2 storage with Cloudflare egress optimization.\n\n## Core Development Tools\n\n### Go (Golang)\n- **Required Version:** 1.25.0 (specified in `go.mod`)\n- **Installed Version:** 1.25.0 linux/amd64\n- **Purpose:** Primary programming language for ARMOR\n- **Source Citation:** `go.mod:3`\n\n### Docker\n- **Installed Version:** 27.5.1\n- **Base Image Used:** golang:1.25-alpine\n- **Purpose:** Container builds and multi-stage deployment\n- **Build Configuration:** `Dockerfile`\n\n### golangci-lint\n- **Configured Version:** 1.25 (in `.golangci.yml`)\n- **Installed:** Not currently in PATH on this development machine\n- **Purpose:** Go linting and static analysis\n- **Enabled Linters:**\n  - `govet` - Go vet static analysis\n  - `ineffassign` - Detect ineffectual assignments\n  - `staticcheck` - Go static analysis\n  - `unused` - Detect unused code\n- **Source Citation:** `.golangci.yml`\n\n### Git\n- **Installed Version:** 2.50.1\n- **Purpose:** Version control\n- **Remote:** github.com/jedarden/armor\n\n## Testing Framework\n\n### Go Testing\n- **Framework:** Built-in Go testing (`go test`)\n- **Test Locations:**\n  - Unit tests: Throughout codebase (standard `*_test.go` files)\n  - Integration tests: `tests/integration/` (requires build tags and credentials)\n- **Test Command:** `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... -short` (used in Docker build)\n- **Source Citation:** `Dockerfile:19`\n\n## Kubernetes Deployment\n\n### Kubernetes\n- **Access Method:** kubectl-proxy over Tailscale (read-only)\n- **Purpose:** Production deployment orchestration\n- **Deployment Files:** `deploy/kubernetes/`\n  - `deployment.yaml` - Main deployment manifest\n  - `service.yaml` - Service definitions\n  - `ingress-dashboard.yaml` - Ingress configuration\n  - `secret.yaml` - Secret management\n  - `kustomization.yaml` - Kustomize configuration\n\n### ArgoCD\n- **Purpose:** GitOps continuous deployment\n- **Access:** Via ardenone-manager cluster\n- **Status:** ARMOR is managed via declarative-config repository\n\n## Build Tools\n\n### go build\n- **Build Command:** `CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags=\"-s -w\" -o /armor ./cmd/armor`\n- **Flags:**\n  - `CGO_ENABLED=0` - Disable CGO for static binary\n  - `GOOS=linux` - Target Linux\n  - `-ldflags=\"-s -w\"` - Strip debug information for smaller binary\n- **Binary Location:** `/armor` (in container)\n\n## Dependency Management\n\n### Go Modules\n- **Module Path:** `github.com/jedarden/armor`\n- **Key Dependencies:**\n  - `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2` v1.41.4 - AWS S3 SDK v2\n  - `github.com/kurin/blazer` v0.5.3 - Google Cloud Storage client\n  - `golang.org/x/crypto` v0.49.0 - Cryptographic primitives\n  - `golang.org/x/sync` v0.12.0 - Concurrency primitives\n- **Source Citation:** `go.mod`\n\n## Current Application Version\n\n- **ARMOR Version:** 0.1.343\n- **Container Image:** ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.343\n- **Version File:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/VERSION`\n- **CI Pipeline:** Auto-bumps VERSION file on every build and publishes to Docker Hub\n\n## Development Workflow\n\n### Local Development\n1. **Build:** `go build ./cmd/armor`\n2. **Test:** `go test ./... -short` (excludes integration tests)\n3. **Lint:** `golangci-lint run` (when installed)\n4. **Vet:** `go vet ./...`\n\n### Docker Build\n1. **Stage 1 (Builder):** golang:1.25-alpine\n   - Downloads dependencies\n   - Runs `go vet` and `go test -short`\n   - Builds static binary\n2. **Stage 2 (Runtime):** scratch\n   - Copies only CA certs, timezone data, and binary\n   - Minimal attack surface\n\n### CI/CD\n- **Platform:** Argo Workflows (iad-ci cluster)\n- **Template:** `armor-build`\n- **Registry:** Docker Hub (ronaldraygun/armor)\n- **GitOps:** ArgoCD on ardenone-manager cluster\n\n## Tool Requirements Summary\n\n| Tool | Required Version | Installed Version | Purpose |\n|------|-----------------|-------------------|---------|\n| Go | 1.25.0 | 1.25.0 | Core language |\n| Docker | Latest | 27.5.1 | Container builds |\n| golangci-lint | Compatible with Go 1.25 | Not in PATH | Linting |\n| Git | Any recent | 2.50.1 | Version control |\n| Kubernetes | N/A (via proxy) | N/A | Deployment |\n\n## Notes\n\n1. **No kubectl in PATH** - Kubernetes access is via kubectl-proxy over Tailscale, not direct kubeconfig\n2. **golangci-lint not installed** - Configured in project but not currently available on this development machine\n3. **Integration tests require credentials** - |\n412\t\n413\t---\n414\t\n415\t## Version Constraint Patterns\n416\t\n417\t### Cargo SemVer Interpretation\n418\t\n419\tIn Cargo.toml, version specifications follow these rules:\n420\t\n421\t| Spec | Meaning | Example |\n422\t|------|---------|---------|\n423\t| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x.x version (≥1.0.0, <2.0.0) | `tokio = \"1\"` accepts 1.52.3 |\n424\t| `\"0.9\"` | Any 0.9.x version (≥0.9.0, <0.10.0) | `serde_yaml = \"0.9\"` accepts 0.9.34 |\n425\t| `\"0.4\"` | Any 0.4.x version (≥0.4.0, <0.5.0) | `chrono = \"0.4\"` accepts 0.4.45 |\n426\t| Exact pinning | Only this specific version | Rarely used in Pluck |\n427\t\n428\t**Key Points:**\n429\t- Cargo automatically selects the latest compatible version\n430\t- `cargo update` upgrades within SemVer bounds\n431\t- Major version bumps require manual Cargo.toml updates\n432\t- Zero-version crates (0.x.y) treat minor versions as major (0.9 → 0.10 is breaking)\n433\t\n434\t---\n435\t\n436\t## Build Compatibility Matrix\n437\t\n438\t### Minimum Build specifically\n483\t```\n484\t\n485\t---\n486\t\n487\t## Dependency Upgrade Guidelines\n488\t\n489\t### Safe Upgrades (within SemVer bounds)\n490\t\n491\tThese can be upgraded automatically by `cargo update`:\n492\t- Patch versions (1.2.3 → 1.2.4) ✅ Safe\n493\t- Minor versions (1.2.x → 1.3.x) for 1.x crates ✅ Safe\n494\t\n495\t### Manual Updates Required\n496\t\n497\tThese require Cargo.toml changes:\n498\t- Major version bumps (1.x → 2.x) ⚠️ Manual review needed\n499\t- Zero-version minor bumps (0.9.x → 0.10.x) ⚠️ Potentially breaking\n500\t\n501\t### Coordinated Updates Required\n502\t\n503\t**OpenTelemetry stack** must be upgraded together:\n504\t- opentelemetry 0.31 → 0.32 (all OTel crates)\n505\t- tonic 0.14 → 0.15 (if required by new OTel)\n506\t- tracing-opentelemetry 0.32 → 0.33 (corresponding bump)\n507\t\n508\t**Never upgrade OTel crates independently** - they must stay in sync.\n509\t\n510\t---\n511\t\n512\t## Known Constraints & Limitations\n513\t\n514\t### OpenTelemetry Version Lock\n515\t\n516\t**Constraint:** All OpenTelemetry crates are locked to version 0.31.x  \n517\t**Reason:** Breaking changes in OpenTelemetry API between major versions  \n518\t**Impact:** Cannot upgrade to 0.32+ without updating all OTel dependencies simultaneously  \n519\t**Workaround:** None - coordinated upgrade required\n520\t\n521\t### Serde YAML Deprecation\n522\t\n523\t**Constraint:** serde_yaml is marked as deprecated  \n524\t**Status:** Still maintained, superseded by newer alternatives  \n525\t**Impact:** None currently - crate is still functional  \n526\t**Future:** May need to migrate to alternative YAML parser in future\n527\t\n528\t### Async Trait Pattern\n529\t\n530\t**Constraint:** async-trait is a proc-macro with compile-time overhead  \n531\t**Impact:** Slower compile times for code with many async traits  \n532\t**Note:** Native async traits in Rust are not yet stable\n533\t\n534\t---\n535\t\n536\t## |\n|------|---------|---------|\n| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x.x version (≥1.0.0, <2.0.0) | `tokio = \"1\"` accepts 1.52.3 |\n| `\"0.9\"` | Any 0.9.x version (≥0.9.0, <0.10.0) | `serde_yaml = \"0.9\"` accepts 0.9.34 |\n| `\"0.4\"` | Any 0.4.x version (≥0.4.0, <0.5.0) | `chrono = \"0.4\"` accepts 0.4.45 |\n| Exact pinning | Only this specific version | Rarely used in Pluck |\n\n**Key Points:**\n- Cargo automatically selects the latest compatible version\n- `cargo update` upgrades within SemVer bounds\n- Major version bumps require manual Cargo.toml updates\n- Zero-version crates (0.x.y) treat minor versions as major (0.9 → 0.10 is breaking)\n\n---\n\n## Build Compatibility Matrix\n\n### Minimum Build Environment\n\n| Component | Minimum | Recommended | Current Installed Safe\n- Minor versions (1.2.x → 1.3.x) for 1.x crates ✅ Safe\n\n### Manual Updates Required\n\nThese require Cargo.toml changes:\n- Major version bumps (1.x → 2.x) ⚠️ Manual review needed\n- Zero-version minor bumps (0.9.x → 0.10.x) ⚠️ Potentially breaking\n\n### Coordinated Updates Required\n\n**OpenTelemetry stack** must be upgraded together:\n- opentelemetry 0.31 → 0.32 (all OTel crates)\n- tonic 0.14 → 0.15 (if required by new OTel)\n- tracing-opentelemetry 0.32 → 0.33 (corresponding bump)\n\n**Never upgrade OTel crates independently** - they must stay in sync.\n\n---\n\n## Deprecation\n\n**Constraint:** serde_yaml is marked as deprecated  \n**Status:** Still maintained, superseded by newer alternatives  \n**Impact:** None currently - crate is still functional version specifications follow these rules:\n\n| Specification | Meaning | Example |\n|--------------|---------|---------|\n| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x.x version (≥1.0.0, <2.0.0) | `tokio = \"1\"` accepts 1.52.3 |\n| `\"0.9\"` | Any 0.9.x version (≥0.9.0, <0.10.0) | `serde_yaml = \"0.9\"` accepts 0.9.34 |\n| `\"0.4\"` | Any 0.4.x version (≥0.4.0, <0.5.0) | `chrono = \"0.4\"` accepts 0.4.45 |\n| `^1.0` | Same as `\"1\"` (caret notation) | Explicit caret requirement |\n\n**Key Points:**\n- Cargo automatically selects the latest compatible version within SemVer bounds\n- `cargo update` upgrades within these bounds\n- Major version bumps require manual Cargo.toml updates\n- Zero-version crates (0.x.y) treat minor versions as major (0.9 → 0.10 is breaking)\n\n### Go Module Versioning\n\nGo modules use semantic versioning with these patterns:\n\n| Specification | Meaning |\n|--------------|---------|\n| `v1.2.3` | Exact version |\n| `v1.2.3+incompatible` | Version without proper semantic versioning |\n| Indirect dependencies | Auto-included in go.sum with checksums |\n\n**Key Points:**\n- go.mod specifies direct dependencies with minimum versions\n- go.sum contains cryptographic checksums for all dependencies\n- Go commands automatically update to latest compatible versions\n- Reproducible builds guaranteed by go.sum verification\n\n---\n\n## System Requirements\n\n### version specifications follow these rules:\n\n| Specification | Meaning | Example |\n|--------------|---------|---------|\n| `\"1\"` | Any 1.x.x version (≥1.0.0, <2.0.0) | `tokio = \"1\"` accepts 1.52.3 |\n| `\"0.9\"` | Any 0.9.x version (≥0.9.0, <0.10.0) | `serde_yaml = \"0.9\"` accepts 0.9.34 |\n| `\"0.4\"` | Any 0.4.x version (≥0.4.0, <0.5.0) | `chrono = \"0.4\"` accepts 0.4.45 |\n| `^1.0` | Same as `\"1\"` (caret notation) | Explicit caret requirement |\n\n**Key Points:**\n- Cargo automatically selects the latest compatible version within SemVer bounds\n- `cargo update` upgrades within these bounds\n- Major version bumps require manual Cargo.toml updates\n- Zero-version crates (0.x.y) treat minor versions as major (0.9 → 0.10 is breaking)\n\n### Go Module Versioning\n\nGo modules use semantic versioning with these patterns:\n\n| Specification | Meaning |\n|--------------|---------|\n| `v1.2.3` | Exact version |\n| `v1.2.3+incompatible` | Version without proper semantic versioning |\n| Indirect dependencies | Auto-included in go.sum with checksums |\n\n**Key Points:**\n- go.mod specifies direct dependencies with minimum versions\n- go.sum contains cryptographic checksums for all dependencies\n- Go commands automatically update to latest compatible versions\n- Reproducible builds guaranteed by go.sum verification\n\n---\n\n## System Requirements\n\n###
